Process_Info.Process_Text_For_AI: as-built Concept Laser Mlab 200R, 200 W fibre laser, wavelength 1070 nm, zig-zag scanning pattern, 90◦ rotation between layers, density > 99.5% ASTM-B572 standard

_evidence.text: A Concept Laser Mlab 200R (GE, Germany) LPBF machine equipped with a 200 W fibre laser source (wavelength = 1070 nm) is employed to print the superalloy. A zig-zag scanning pattern is applied, along with 90◦rotation in the laser path between layers (Fig. 1 d). All specimens are fabricated under optimised printing parameters (Table 1), resulting in a high density exceeding 99.5 % (measured by the Archimedes method), without any microcracks or lack of fusion defects being found in the AB state.

the solid-solution heat treatment is conducted at 1150 ◦C for 2 h (based on the ASTM-B572 standard) to eliminate the LPBF-induced residual stresses and initial dislocations (ASTM, 2016), without altering the grain morphology (demonstrated in subsequent Section 3.2.1).
Uniaxial tensile tests at RT condition are performed using an Exceed E44-30 kN (MTS, China) electronic universal testing machine with a strain rate of 0.02 min-1, in accordance with the ASTM E8
YS and EL (defined as the fracture EL) of AB-H are found to be 1121.5 ± 9 MPa and 8.2 ± 0.72 % in average
